title: Correctness, Completeness, Optimality, Metrics, and Selection · Language Clinic: Correctness Witness
summary: A solver wins from one timing, path length is compared across different mazes, and explored-cell animation is mistaken for useful work. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the correctness witness increment in solver benchmark and decision report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate endpoints, adjacency, passability, uniqueness policy, and reported cost against an independent route checker.
Compare route validity, total cost, expansions, frontier peak, heuristic zero case, unreachable case, and changed weights.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Predict Correctness Witness · Theory Correctness Witness
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory puzzle team handles case 2801 during a classroom algorithm comparison. Build deterministic Python evidence to compare solver correctness, completeness, optimality, work, memory, and selection on identical fixtures; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates.

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-correctness-witness — Validate endpoints, adjacency, passability, uniqueness policy, and reported cost against an independent route checker.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-solver-evidence-comparison-3
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-solver-evidence-comparison-3.
# Case 5798: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def worked_maze_solver_evidence_comparison_3(fixtures: int = 24, valid_results: int = 24, solvers: int = 4, changed_cases: int = 8, isolated_metrics: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "gridworked3",
        "topology5798",
        "search3dekrow",
        "eventworked35798",
        "access5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if fixtures < 8 or valid_results < 0:
        return False, valid_results, "benchmark-fixtures"
    if valid_results != fixtures:
        return False, valid_results, "invalid-solver-result"
    if solvers < 4 or changed_cases < 4:
        return False, solvers, "solver-coverage"
    if not isolated_metrics:
        return False, changed_cases, "confounded-metrics"
    assert valid_results == fixtures
    return True, valid_results, "defensible-solver-comparison"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python maze decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected grid, topology, frontier, path, callback, state, accessibility, save, package, or recovery case, then state which Tcl, Tk, tkinter, Canvas, display, focus, input, assistive-technology, filesystem, timing, installer, or production claim still needs controlled native transfer evidence.

Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-correctness-witness-1qv8ph
# Competency: maze-correctness-witness.
# Case 1945: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: all graph claims need an independent witness.
# Changed case: replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path.
def evidence_maze_correctness_witness_1qv8ph(fixtures: int = 24, valid_results: int = 24, solvers: int = 4, changed_cases: int = 8, isolated_metrics: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1qv8ph = "1qv8ph-hp8vq1-1945"
    assert evidence_variant_1qv8ph
    evidence_axes_1qv8ph = (
        "grid1qv8ph",
        "topology1945",
        "searchhp8vq1",
        "event1qv8ph1945",
        "access19451qv8ph",
        "recoveryhp8vq11945",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1qv8ph)) == 6
    if fixtures < 8 or valid_results < 0:
        return False, valid_results, "benchmark-fixtures"
    if valid_results != fixtures:
        return False, valid_results, "invalid-solver-result"
    if solvers < 4 or changed_cases < 4:
        return False, solvers, "solver-coverage"
    if not isolated_metrics:
        return False, changed_cases, "confounded-metrics"
    assert valid_results == fixtures
    return True, valid_results, "defensible-solver-comparison"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Validate endpoints, adjacency, passability, uniqueness policy, and reported cost against an independent route checker.
